remember: Students will be able to recall and state the basic definitions and concepts of statistics.
Students will be able to list and explain the steps involved in collecting, organizing, and presenting data.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different types of graphs and charts used to represent data.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of data collection and analysis in real-life situations.
Students will be able to explain the concept of frequency distribution and its relevance in statistics.
Students will be able to understand the concept of measures of central tendency and their importance in data analysis.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of statistics to real-life problems and situations.
Students will be able to calculate and interpret the mean, median, and mode of a set of data.
Students will be able to create and interpret frequency distributions, histograms, and box plots.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze and interpret data presented in various forms, including graphs and charts.
Students will be able to identify and explain patterns and trends in data sets.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different data sets using statistical measures.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of statistics and its importance in real-life situations.
To be able to collect, organize, and present data in various forms.
To understand and apply the concepts of mean, median, and mode in data analysis.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate and interpret the mean, median, and mode of a set of data.
To create and interpret frequency distributions, histograms, and box plots.
To analyze and interpret data presented in various forms, including graphs and charts.
